_source: Trajectory field note: Multi-LoRA Training for Continual Learning
_hardware: 1x H200 node: 4 inference GPUs + 4 training GPUs, model=Qwen3-4B-Instruct-2507
_workload: 8 concurrent LoRA adapter-training experiments, 10 RL training steps each, GSM8K benchmark
_note: Final=makespan(all done), Mean=mean JCT, First=first experiment completes (latency), Step=wall-clock per multiplexed step

Serial_single_lora_N1_baseline._note: Single-LoRA (no sharing) vs multi-LoRA shared base — 8% overhead

Concurrent_multi_lora_N2._note: Ideal operating point per article: only 15% rollout overhead, low latency regression

Concurrent_multi_lora_N8._note: Max throughput point. First-experiment latency regresses 1.97x vs serial.

step_decomposition_N8._note: 3.81x is training SUB-STEP only. Full step = 500s (2.62x). Rollout = 77% of total increase.

article_open_problems: Dynamic load balancing across jobs (P1) — our scheduler's target

our_calibration_correction.bug: Old calibration used training-sub-step scaling (3.81x) as full step time. Fixed to use article Table 4.3 step times directly.
